old_claim.note: Pottery provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No pottery dependency remains because the directly documented Lascaux lamps are shaped sandstone and hollow limestone plates rather than ceramic vessels.

source_shape.source_locator: Lighting section, paragraph 4: describes a crafted red-sandstone lamp and more than one hundred hollow limestone fuel plates at Lascaux.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The surviving Lascaux lighting vessels are stone lamps and plates, not pottery containers.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Ceramic lamps exist elsewhere and later, but pottery is absent from the physical lamp evidence that now defines this node.

ontology_before: A generic ceramic technology was treated as an enabler of all oil lamps despite the cited source only documenting early pottery.
ontology_after: The Lascaux lamp claim follows its directly evidenced stone construction without a ceramic prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: oil_lamps no longer directly depends on pottery.
would_reject_if: The Lascaux objects anchoring this node were reidentified as ceramic lamps rather than shaped sandstone and limestone.
short_reason: The scoped lamps are stone, not pottery.
